subject: Update aai_keystore and ext pem
commit_message:
Update aai_keystore and ext pem

Updated AAF config to use newly issued certificate in traversal

Issue-ID: AAI-2286
Change-Id: Idb9e9370a5e04057594658598c0c38384dd9f0ca
Signed-off-by: Jimmy Forsyth <jf2512@att.com>

Patch Set 4:

@Yang Xu / integration team - is there an existing environment I can use to test this?  My dev environment where i usually do this is broken and the AAI tenant space has nothing currently

Patch Set 4:

I think there's a circular dependency here.  The root aai pod waits for traversal and resources to be up before init, but those pods need the pem file that the root pod mounts for them.  In my environment the aai pod never comes up and traversal and resources are in CrashLoopBackoff

Patch Set 4:

> @Yang Xu / integration team - is there an existing environment I
 > can use to test this?  My dev environment where i usually do this
 > is broken and the AAI tenant space has nothing currently

Jimmy, you can use Integration-SB-05 to test. Integration-SB-05 has 3.0.1 release, but it should be ok to test your change

Patch Set 4:

> @Yang Xu / integration team - is there an existing environment I
 > can use to test this?  My dev environment where i usually do this
 > is broken and the AAI tenant space has nothing currently

Jimmy, I asked around and someone is using SB-05 for their test. Is it possible to preserve AAI data before you make change and restore it after?  Or you can use SB-02 which deploys master branch ONAP

Patch Set 4:

if you use the magic word (oom_redeploy) from an OOM patchest, it will trigger the full deployement of a full ONAP + test for you

this will be based on the current OOM master branch + your patchset

See https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/OOM+Gating

Note as I put the magic word in this comment, it will ask for new gating based on the last available version of this patchset

Abandoned

Going to leave out the AAF props changes and get the main keystore updated to unblock testing
